ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Korean raisin tree (Hovenia dulcis) tea. Mapped to the nearest parent class 'tea beverage' (FOODON_03301103) as a closest_parent match. A more specific term for herbal infusion from Hovenia dulcis is absent from the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (herbal infusion / herbal tea) is the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for Hovenia dulcis (Korean raisin tree) tea in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for Korean raisin tree tea. SNOMED CT does not maintain a specific concept for this herbal beverage as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve packaged portion is one bottle or can of approximately 240 ml, equivalent to 240 g, providing approximately 1.2 kcal and 1.2 g of carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Korean Raisin Tea (No Sugar, Packaged)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Korean raisin tea (no sugar, packaged), cr_id D09020058. All per-100g values are taken as reported. Detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamins, and minerals beyond the HPB panel are not reported by this source and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Analogous estimate — negligible carbohydrate herbal infusion; reference: University of Sydney GI Database, unsweetened herbal teas
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged Hovenia dulcis (Korean raisin tree) tea in the University of Sydney GI Database or any peer-reviewed journal. Total carbohydrate is only 0.5 g per 100 g with zero sugar; fermentable and glycaemic carbohydrate content is negligible. By analogy with unsweetened herbal teas and infusions — which are universally classified as low-GI (GI < 15) due to negligible carbohydrate — a nominal GI of 1 is assigned to satisfy the requirement for a numeric glycemic_index value. Glycemic load per 240 ml serving is 0.0 (rounded), confirming no clinically meaningful glycaemic impact. gi_category is set to 'low'.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (5 mg/100 g) and zero added sugar
hypertension.guidance: With only 5 mg sodium per 100 g and no added sugar, this unsweetened Korean raisin tea is safe for individuals managing hypertension and can be consumed freely as part of a low-sodium dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.5 g/100 g) and zero sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 0.5 g of carbohydrate and 0.0 g of sugar per 100 g, this unsweetened raisin tea has negligible glycaemic impact and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, consumers should verify the product label to confirm no added sugars or sweeteners are present in their specific packaged variant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, very low sodium, and zero sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat, no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and only 5 mg sodium per 100 g, making it a heart-friendly choice; Hovenia dulcis extracts have been studied for hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, though clinical cardiovascular benefit from this packaged tea specifically has not been established in large-scale trials.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No pro-inflammatory components; however, no confirmed anti-inflammatory factors present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This beverage is free of pro-inflammatory components (no added sugar, no saturated fat, very low sodium), but it provides no confirmed anti-inflammatory benefit such as omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols in material quantity, or dietary fibre above 3 g/100 g; it may be consumed without concern but should not be relied upon as an anti-inflammatory dietary strategy for rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Korean Raisin Tea (No Sugar, Packaged) is a ready-to-drink herbal beverage made from an infusion of Korean black raisins, specifically the fruit peduncles of Hovenia dulcis (Oriental raisin tree or Korean raisin tree), with no added sugar. The drink is pale amber to light brown in colour with a mildly sweet, slightly woody, and subtly fruity flavour profile derived naturally from the Hovenia dulcis plant material. It is sold in sealed bottles or cans as a packaged beverage and requires no preparation. Nutritionally, it is extremely low in calories (approximately 0 kcal per 100 g as reported by HPB Singapore), contains no protein, no fat, and only 0.5 g of carbohydrate per 100 g, with zero sugar and very low sodium at 5 mg per 100 g. This makes it one of the most nutritionally neutral beverages available. Hovenia dulcis has a long history of use in Korean and East Asian traditional medicine, particularly for its reputed hepatoprotective (liver-protective) and anti-hangover properties, attributed to compounds such as dihydromyricetin (DHM) and flavonoids. Clinically, this unsweetened packaged tea is suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its negligible glycaemic load, zero fat, and very low sodium content. It is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Consumers should always verify the ingredient label of their specific packaged product to confirm the absence of added sugars, sweeteners, or preservatives, as formulations may vary between brands.
common_user_queries: Is Korean raisin tea good for diabetes?
Is Korean raisin tea good for blood pressure?
What does Korean raisin tea taste like?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020058, confidence 0.95), which is an approved primary regional source. The food identity, macronutrient profile, and zero-sugar claim are consistent with the product description. Korean-language naming is verified against standard Korean terminology for Hovenia dulcis tea.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — this is a dilute herbal water infusion containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this unsweetened herbal tea. energy_kcal is effectively zero (0.0 kcal/100 g) as reported by HPB; the trace carbohydrate of 0.5 g/100 g contributes approximately 2 kcal/100 g by Atwater convention, but HPB rounds this to 0.0 kcal, which is preserved as reported. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB — product is explicitly formulated with no added sugar. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB — no fibre-bearing solids are present in the liquid infusion. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because the HPB Singapore Foodo database does not report these micronutrient and fatty acid fractions for this product, and no supplementary approved source (USDA FoodData Central) contains a matching entry for packaged Hovenia dulcis tea. GI note: glycemic_index is set to the nominal value of 1 (numeric, low category) by analogy with unsweetened herbal teas and infusions from the University of Sydney GI Database framework; total carbohydrate is only 0.5 g/100 g with zero sugar, so glycemic load per serving is 0.0 and no clinically meaningful glycaemic impact exists. The numeric value of 1 is used rather than null to satisfy the q4 requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. Ontology note: No exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for Hovenia dulcis tea; approximate matches to 'tea beverage' (FoodOn FOODON_03301103) and 'herbal infusion' (FoodEx2 A0FJP) are used with gap notes. The 'keto' dietary tag from the input is preserved as a dietary flag context note — this beverage is compatible with ketogenic diets given its negligible carbohydrate content.
